abla_	heta L(	heta)
&=-\sum_{a} R(a) 
abla_	heta \pi_	heta(a), \
&=-\sum_{a} R(a) \pi_	heta(a) 
abla_	heta \log \pi_	heta(a), \
&=-\mathbb{E}_{a \sim \pi_	heta}\left[R(a) 
abla_	heta \log \pi_{	heta}(a) ight]. what the second derivative of the gradient?

Starting from
\[
\nabla_\theta L(\theta) \;=\; -\sum_a R(a)\,\pi_\theta(a)\,\nabla_\theta \log \pi_\theta(a),
\]
the “second derivative of the gradient” is the Hessian
\[
\nabla_\theta^2 L(\theta)\in \mathbb{R}^{d\times d}.
\]

### Discrete sum form
Differentiate once more (product rule). Using \(\nabla_\theta \pi_\theta(a)=\pi_\theta(a)\nabla_\theta \log\pi_\theta(a)\):
\[
\nabla_\theta^2 L(\theta)
= -\sum_a R(a)\,\nabla_\theta\!\left(\pi_\theta(a)\,\nabla_\theta \log \pi_\theta(a)\right)
\]
\[
= -\sum_a R(a)\Big[
(\nabla_\theta \pi_\theta(a))(\nabla_\theta \log \pi_\theta(a))^\top
\;+\;\pi_\theta(a)\,\nabla_\theta^2 \log \pi_\theta(a)
\Big]
\]
\[
= -\sum_a R(a)\,\pi_\theta(a)\Big[
\nabla_\theta \log \pi_\theta(a)\,\nabla_\theta \log \pi_\theta(a)^\top
\;+\;\nabla_\theta^2 \log \pi_\theta(a)
\Big].
\]

### Expectation form
Equivalently,
\[
\nabla_\theta^2 L(\theta)
= -\mathbb{E}_{a\sim \pi_\theta}\Big[
R(a)\Big(
\nabla_\theta \log \pi_\theta(a)\,\nabla_\theta \log \pi_\theta(a)^\top
+\nabla_\theta^2 \log \pi_\theta(a)
\Big)
\Big].
\]

### If \(R\) also depends on \(\theta\)
If \(R=R_\theta(a)\), extra terms appear:
\[
\nabla_\theta^2 L
= -\mathbb{E}_{a\sim\pi_\theta}\Big[
R_\theta(a)\big(\nabla\log\pi\,\nabla\log\pi^\top+\nabla^2\log\pi\big)
+(\nabla R_\theta(a))\,\nabla\log\pi^\top
+ \nabla\log\pi\,(\nabla R_\theta(a))^\top
+ \nabla^2 R_\theta(a)
\Big],
\]
(where all gradients/Hessians are w.r.t. \(\theta\)).
